text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech-led bounce, semis surged. VIX fell to 15.8 despite hawkish CPI data, signaling positioning-driven risk-on. Softer core MoM CPI (-0.17pp) triggered short-covering in semis and tech while energy collapsed on oil’s -2.85% drop.

INDICES — VIX 15.8, down. Rankings: SOXX +3.50%, QQQ +1.66%, IWM +1.28%, SPY +0.85%, DIA +0.27%. Large > small by 0.38pp. Silver +2.48% led metals; oil -2.85% was the cross-asset laggard. Neither SPY nor QQQ below their 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.9x, QQQ 0.9x — both modestly below average, yet QQQ price up = low-conviction rally. SOXX at 0.9x with +3.50% is mild accumulation. XLY saw 1.1x elevated volume with a +1.34% gain, indicating genuine demand.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +2.18%, XLY Discretionary +1.34%, XLF Financials +1.04%, XLC Comms +0.96%, XLI Industrials +0.38%, XLB Materials +0.20%, XLRE Real Estate +0.18%, XLV Healthcare -0.08%, XLU Utilities -0.51%, XLE Energy -1.40%, XLP Staples -1.41%. Top Industries: SILJ +4.54% (Silver miners) and DRAM +3.74% (semis). Bottom: UNG -6.64% (Nat Gas) and USO -2.85% (Oil). Money flows from energy/staples into tech/semis/natural-resources — label: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — QQQ put-call ratio 1.23 elevated, IV skew +10.7% = fear hedging. SPY skew only +3.0%, PCR 0.90 neutral. VIX at 15.8 below its 20-day MA of 17.1 = complacency relative to recent levels. GEX flip for QQQ at 738, spot near — dealer hedging could amplify any move lower.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil -2.85% dragging XLE down 1.40% while silver +2.48% and gold +1.00% rallied, diverging from energy — metals benefitting from dollar flat and CPI-driven yield curve steepening.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 66.8 Greed. Top movers: UNG -6.64% (Nat Gas vol spike 2.3x = distribution), SOXX +3.50% (semis), SLV +2.48% (silver). 20d momentum extremes: best sector = XLF +6.87%, worst = XLE -6.02%. UNG’s vol spike at 2.3x with price down 6.64% signals institutional selling. Watch: UNG — distribution with vol spike, CIBR — vol 1.8x suggesting cybersecurity accumulation, SLV — trend divergence vs oil.
